calculation.method: close from Yahoo Finance ^GSPC historical data; one-day percent move computed against the June 15 close of 7,554.29; Investopedia reported a 0.6% decline

text: The S&P 500 closed at 7,511.35 on June 16, 2026, down 0.57% from its June 15 close of 7,554.29, as a tech-led pullback followed Monday's rally.

calculation.method: close from Yahoo Finance ^IXIC historical data; one-day percent move computed against the June 15 close of 26,683.94; Investopedia reported a 1.2% decline

text: The Nasdaq Composite closed at 26,376.34 on June 16, 2026, down 1.15%, the weakest of the major US indexes as chip stocks led the tech sector lower.

calculation.method: close from Yahoo Finance ^DJI historical data; one-day percent move computed against the June 15 close of 51,671.03; Investopedia reported a 0.6% gain to a record high

text: The Dow Jones Industrial Average closed at 51,999.67 on June 16, 2026, up 0.64% to a record high for a second straight session, diverging from the lower S&P 500 and Nasdaq.

calculation.method: close from Yahoo Finance and Investing.com VIX historical data; one-day percent move against the June 15 close of 16.20; Investing.com labels the June 16 change +1.30% and the June 15 close 16.20 (-8.37%)

text: The Cboe Volatility Index (VIX) closed at 16.41 on June 16, 2026, up 1.30% from its June 15 close of 16.20. The 16.20 level (down 8.37%) was the June 15 close, not June 16; the fear gauge ticked higher on the day but stayed at a low, calm level.

market_session: CNN Fear & Greed Index reading for the June 16, 2026 US session (MacroMicro daily series); classified in the 'Fear' zone

calculation.method: MacroMicro daily series value for 2026-06-16; prior-session reference about 41 at the June 15 close from FinHacker

text: The CNN Fear & Greed Index stood at 41.29 ('Fear') for June 16, 2026, little changed from about 41 at the June 15 close and still in the Fear zone despite Monday's rally.

On June 16, 2026 the year's biggest winners led the declines - the PHLX Semiconductor Index sold off sharply (Investopedia's wrap cited a drop of nearly 6%), with Intel and AMD each down more than 6% and Nvidia off more than 2% - while industrials and financials gained and the Dow set a record, pointing to a rotation out of crowded tech rather than broad risk aversion.
